entries.definition:
j–r–r to draw, to pull, to drag along; track, trail, galaxy; crime, guilt; to ruminate. Of this root, يَجُرُّ yajurr occurs once in the Qurʾan.
يَجُرُّ yajurr [imperf. of v. جَرَّ jarra, trans.] to drag, to pull, to draw (7:150) وَأَخَذَ بِرَأْسِ أَخِيهِ يَجُرُّهُ إِلَيْهِ he threw the tablets and took hold of the head of his brother, dragging him to him.
entries.definition_english:
j–r–r to draw, to pull, to drag along; track, trail, galaxy; crime, guilt; to ruminate. Of this root, يَجُرُّ yajurr occurs once in the Qurʾan.
يَجُرُّ yajurr [imperf. of v. جَرَّ jarra, trans.] to drag, to pull, to draw (7:150) وَأَخَذَ بِرَأْسِ أَخِيهِ يَجُرُّهُ إِلَيْهِ he threw the tablets and took hold of the head of his brother, dragging him to him.
